reviews.review_description: Loved it from the start
Amazing very immersive ￼

I really wanted to like this. I bought it and forgot about it until now. Fired it up and the intro was good but after that it’s really jarring how poor the graphics are. Really low quality images, 3D models have bad aliasing.

Then at what I assume is the ending, there was music but I’m stuck in a black screen. This is on a Quest 3.

If I’d have played it within the 14 day period this would be refunded!

My parents lived through this time. To go back  to this monumental occasion. Back in time to one of the greatest engineering miracles,marvels in the history of man was truly an honor! It was brilliant! Touching!
Really enjoyed it and love showing it to family and friends as a great example of what the Quest is used for.

I don't even  know where to begin this game is awesome, you can rock a rocket, explore the moon, be on the rocket, holy cow. The texture is realistic, their mouths move as they talk, and I sent a photo to a friend and he though I was actually on the moon. Def recommend.
Waste of money

reviews.review_title: If you're thinking about it DO IT
reviews.review_description: This was the best éducation app I've seen in a while it was very enjoyable, It was worth every penny and more. I wish i was taught like this in school, I probably would have become an astronaut.  Thank you to the creators of this app.

Bad graphics, like REALLY BAD graphics. Extremely short for the amount of money you pay. If this was 1998, I would maybe see the value. There is only one part where you can interact, and this is not really that educational or informative as the reviews led me to believe. If you’re on the fence about getting this like I was, it is worth a skip
This is a lovely App . Every family should have this .

I was SO disappointed with the Meta Quest version of this. I also have the Playstation version, which gives you some fun control options for precision landing, such as responsive thruster control, and a readout for ground speed so you could monitor your progress for slowing down. There was also a set of Landing Point Designator (LPD) number lines on the window to allow you to judge your angle and altitude. ALL of that has been removed from the Quest version. The Quest version only lets you squirt the thruster quickly about every 3 seconds, which is useless. (And instead of a satisfying engine rumble, you get an annoying little toot sound). The flight mechanics and physics seem to be all messed up (if you turn the LEM sideways to slow down, the lander starts ascending instead). There is no ground speed on the flight computer, only altitude. The LPD lines on the window are almost invisible. And the worst thing of all is the non-stop "inspirational" music in the background, which YOU CAN'T TURN OFF. I don't understand why this experience was dumbed-down so much, and so much more inferior to the PlayStation version. Please fix it! At least get rid of that annoying music in the background!

reviews.review_title: I don’t think I needed a history lesson
reviews.review_description:
I thought the graphics where good and all but for paying 10 dollars the developers should fix the interactive part of the game as well as add more too it, I feel that it’s quite dumbed down, I mean do they show in the trailer of the game that you can’t see your body or hand’s the whole time no, no they don’t because all they care about is hyping up there game to get the most money out of people when really there are 
free games that are way funnier and interactive!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
All I can say is I cried. Amazing I only spent 10.$ after seeing it I would have spent 50.$
If I could give it a ten I would
I really loved and enjoyed this wonderful experience.  Absolutely worth it

reviews.review_title: Fails completely as an Edutainment title
reviews.review_description:
The scales are off, when your sitting, and you should sit with those camera movements. The interactive bits are either unresponsive or unclear what to do. The physics when undocking and dropping to the moon are completely off. And there is no commentary whatsoever.
And to top it all it let's you listen to JFK's "Not because it's easy" speech for over 5 minutes, which is about 3 too long.
Not recommended!

The premise of this experience is fantastic, who wouldn't want to experience the Apollo 11 launch, landing and return? But the developers doesn't manage to do it justice. The switching between stationary and moving camera with no warning can feel a bit dizzying at times, the navigation of the menu system is awful, but the graphics is the worst thing about this experience. Not only are the textures incredibly low resolution which breaks the immersion and ruins any feeling of amazement you wished for, but the application also uses really hard fixed foveted rendering which becomes extremely noticable in the sparse environments. Don't buy this experience, just wait for something like Mission ISS to come to the Quest and hope they did a better job at optimizing it for the platform than these developers did with their application.

I first played the Apollo 11 Experience on the PSVR and I was very positively surprised how immersive the experience it was. The experience itself is written, directed and edited with a high standard and the same is true for the Quest version.
Unfortunately the same can not be said for the quality of the graphics, sound and gameplay. The textures have been downgraded to the point where some of them just look cartoonish and downright ugly. (Spoiler Alert) For example, the camera flight from the top of the Saturn V rocket on the launchpad has virtually no detail left on the rocket body itself or the launch pad and tower to the point where it is simply no longer interesting to look at; it looks more like a story board drawing or a cell-shaded sketch then a 3D model of the Saturn V. Inside the spacecraft the detail is slightly better, but the aliasing is very apparent. Even on the moon surface the textures are so bad that the simulation just can not deliver an immersive experience. I believe this could have done a lot better; other VR experiences like Nature Treks VR for example show what can be done even with the limited power of the Snapdragon processor.
Add to this some flaws in the gameplay (the game can't really cope with the fact that you can move yourself in 6DOF without using their locomotion / teleport system) and the illusion is destroyed. 

So the verdict is disappointing.  The source material is great and remains great on the Quest. But the simulation would have needed much more polishing and optimization to deliver the best possible experience.

Although it was specified in the description, absolutely no interactivity at all in this app.
Plus, on the approach of the moon, a big texture seam effect kill all the ambiance.
It's a shame, really, considering how the PC version was good.
Great potential, but poor realization.
PS : the disky does not even throw the 1201 and 1202 errors on it's screen when they occur, could have  been a lot more imersive.
If you’re even remotely into astronomy and science, this app is really unbelievable. Really puts ￼you in the seat of the astronaut. ￼ highly recommend!
